question: Is the NuScale overall safety case from Ch.01 adequately supported by the detailed engineering in Ch.05, considering both tabular data and diagrams?

nuscale_ch01_0069.title: Piping and Instrumentation Diagrams
nuscale_ch01_0069.summary: This section describes the use of simplified electrical, instrumentation and control (I&C), and mechanical drawings in the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report to visually clarify design information. It lists the relevant diagrams and legends used for both I&C and piping and instrumentation diagrams, and notes that site-specific diagrams and legends will be provided by COL applicants referencing the NuScale design certification.

nuscale_ch01_0068.title: Electrical and Instrumentation and Control Drawings
nuscale_ch01_0068.summary: This section describes the use of simplified electrical, instrumentation, and control (I&C) drawings in the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report to visually clarify design information alongside written text. It references tables and figures that list and explain the symbols and diagrams used, and notes that site-specific diagrams and legends will be provided by applicants referencing the NuScale design certification.

nuscale_ch01_0558.title: DSRS 12.3-12.4, Rev 0: Radiation Protection Design FeaturesII.9 RG 8.15
nuscale_ch01_0558.summary: This section summarizes NuScale’s conformance with various regulatory guides related to radiation protection design features, as outlined in DSRS 12.3-12.4, Rev 0. It details whether each guide is applicable, fully conformed to, or only partially conformed to, with references to supporting sections and tables for further information.

nuscale_ch01_0557.title: DSRS 12.3-12.4, Rev 0: Radiation Protection Design FeaturesII.8 RG 8.10
nuscale_ch01_0557.summary: This section summarizes NuScale’s conformance with various regulatory guides related to radiation protection design features, as outlined in DSRS 12.3-12.4. It details whether each regulatory guide is applicable, not applicable, conforms, or partially conforms, with references to relevant sections for further information. The table provides a structured overview of compliance status for each guide, supporting the overall safety analysis report.

nuscale_ch01_0682.title: DSRS 15.1.5, Rev 0: Steam System Piping Failures Inside and Outside of ContainmentII.4 AssumptionsWorst Case Failure of Single Active Component
nuscale_ch01_0682.summary: This section summarizes NuScale’s conformance with regulatory criteria for steam system piping failures inside and outside containment, specifically addressing the assumption of the worst-case failure of a single active component. It confirms that NuScale’s analysis and safety classifications meet the requirements outlined in DSRS 15.1.5, with no exceptions noted.

nuscale_ch01_0020.title: Safety Considerations
nuscale_ch01_0020.summary: The NuScale design enhances safety by maintaining a vacuum in the containment vessel (CNV), which reduces corrosion, aids leak detection, and limits combustible gases. Its passive safety systems and integral design eliminate large-break loss-of-coolant accident (LOCA) scenarios and reduce reliance on external power, resulting in a lower core damage frequency compared to conventional reactors.

nuscale_ch01_0663.title: DSRS 15.0.3, Rev 0: Design Basis Accident Radiological Consequence Analyses for the NuScale SMR DesignII.3 Technical Support Center Radiological Habitability
nuscale_ch01_0663.summary: This section summarizes the NuScale SMR’s conformance with regulatory criteria for radiological consequence analyses during design basis accidents. It states that the Technical Support Center (TSC) meets dose acceptance criteria when AC power is available, but TSC functions are transferred to the main control room if AC power is lost. Overall, the analyses largely conform to regulatory standards, with partial conformance noted for TSC habitability under certain conditions.

nuscale_ch05_0013.title: References
nuscale_ch05_0013.summary: This section lists references and standards used in the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report regarding the integrity of the reactor coolant boundary. It cites guidelines and reports from the Electric Power Research Institute and the American Society of Mechanical Engineers, as well as specific ASME code cases relevant to reactor component materials, quality assurance, and inspection procedures.

nuscale_ch01_0662.title: DSRS 15.0.3, Rev 0: Design Basis Accident Radiological Consequence Analyses for the NuScale SMR DesignII.2 Control Room Radiological Habitability
nuscale_ch01_0662.summary: This section summarizes NuScale’s compliance with regulatory criteria for radiological consequence analyses during design basis accidents, specifically addressing control room radiological habitability. It confirms that the NuScale SMR design meets the relevant requirements for protecting control room personnel from radiological exposure during postulated accidents, as outlined in DSRS 15.0.3 and related sections. The document also notes partial conformance for the Technical Support Center, with full compliance when AC power is available.

nuscale_ch01_0393.title: DSRS 9.5.2, Rev 0: Communication SystemsII.2 Onsite Technical Support Center and Operational Support Center
nuscale_ch01_0393.summary: This section summarizes NuScale’s conformance with DSRS 9.5.2 requirements for communication systems in emergency support centers. The NuScale standard plant design partially meets criteria for onsite technical and operational support centers and emergency facilities, but the responsibility for communication systems and related emergency response equipment lies with the COL applicant. Certain aspects, such as site-specific construction and ITAAC preparation, are also designated as COL applicant responsibilities.

nuscale_ch01_0392.title: DSRS 9.5.2, Rev 0: Communication SystemsII.1 Emergency Facilities and Equipment
nuscale_ch01_0392.summary: This section outlines NuScale's conformance with regulatory criteria for fire protection and emergency communication systems. It notes partial conformance with certain fire protection criteria due to the modular design's limitations, while clarifying that some requirements are the responsibility of Combined License (COL) applicants rather than the design certification applicant. Risk-informed, performance-based fire protection programs and operational implementation milestones are also designated as COL applicant responsibilities.

nuscale_ch01_0066.title: Material Referenced
nuscale_ch01_0066.summary: This section lists the topical and technical reports incorporated by reference in the NuScale Power Plant Design Certification Application, as detailed in Tables 1.6-1 and 1.6-2. These reports cover various aspects such as quality assurance, risk significance, safety classification, accident analysis, and methodology qualifications, and are referenced throughout specific sections of the Final Safety Analysis Report (FSAR).

nuscale_ch01_0611.title: SRP 13.4, Rev 3: Operational ProgramsNot Applicable Various (Including Attachment, Sample FSAR Table 13.4-x)
nuscale_ch01_0611.summary: This section outlines NuScale's conformance with various regulatory criteria related to emergency planning and operational programs. Many requirements are designated as the responsibility of the Combined License (COL) applicant, with NuScale’s design partially or fully conforming to some criteria and supporting exemptions for others. Specifically, for SRP 13.4, Rev 3: Operational Programs, there are no specific requirements applicable to NuScale at this stage.

nuscale_ch01_0134.title: 8.40 Methods for Measuring Effective Dose Equivalent from External Exposure
nuscale_ch01_0134.summary: Section 8.40 states that the guidance for methods of measuring effective dose equivalent from external radiation exposure is not applicable to the NuScale facility, as these dosimetry methods are the responsibility of the COL applicant or licensee. Therefore, NuScale does not address this guidance in its Final Safety Analysis Report.

nuscale_ch05_0006.title: System Evaluation
nuscale_ch05_0006.summary: This section describes the evaluation of the NuScale Power Plant’s systems to optimize performance across various power levels. Key operational parameters—such as coolant temperatures, flow rates, and pressures—are established and verified through both software modeling and hand calculations, accounting for uncertainties and ensuring efficient, safe operation within design limits. The system’s natural circulation design minimizes pressure losses, and uncertainties are addressed using test data and design requirements.

nuscale_ch01_0632.title: SRP 14.3.2, Rev 0: Structural and Systems Engineering - Inspections, Tests, Analyses, and Acceptance CriteriaAll Various
nuscale_ch01_0632.summary: This section summarizes NuScale’s conformance with various regulatory criteria related to inspections, tests, analyses, and acceptance criteria (ITAAC) for structural and systems engineering. It details which regulatory requirements are fully or partially met, and notes when certain sections are not applicable, often because the methodology is addressed elsewhere or the criteria pertain only to specific applicants or amendment types.

nuscale_ch01_0747.title: SRP 15.4.6, Rev 2: Inadvertent Decrease in Boron Concentration in the Reactor Coolant System (PWR)II.1 Reactor Coolant and Main Steam System Pressures
nuscale_ch01_0747.summary: This section summarizes NuScale’s conformance with SRP 15.4.6, Rev 2, regarding inadvertent decreases in boron concentration in the reactor coolant system for PWRs. It states that NuScale’s design meets the relevant regulatory criteria for reactor coolant and main steam system pressures, fuel cladding integrity, incident frequency, and analysis methods, while noting that operator action and certain non-safety system guidance are not applicable due to design differences such as the absence of reactor coolant pumps.

nuscale_ch01_0639.title: SRP 14.3.9, (March 2007): Human Factors Engineering - Inspections, Tests, Analyses, and Acceptance CriteriaAll Various
nuscale_ch01_0639.summary: This section summarizes NuScale’s conformance with various sections of the Standard Review Plan (SRP) and Design Specific Review Standard (DSRS) regarding Inspections, Tests, Analyses, and Acceptance Criteria (ITAAC), including Human Factors Engineering. For most SRP sections, the methodology for developing ITAAC is referenced as being provided in SRP 14.3, and these sections are marked as not applicable, except for Physical Security Hardware, which is only partially addressed.

nuscale_ch05_0004.title: System Description
nuscale_ch05_0004.summary: The Reactor Coolant System (RCS) in the NuScale design is located inside the containment vessel and includes components such as the reactor pressure vessel, pressurizer, and associated piping and controls. It removes heat from the reactor core to the steam generators using natural circulation, and features systems for degasification and emergency core cooling to maintain safety and integrity of the reactor coolant pressure boundary.

nuscale_ch01_0748.title: SRP 15.4.6, Rev 2: Inadvertent Decrease in Boron Concentration in the Reactor Coolant System (PWR)II.2 Fuel Cladding Integrity
nuscale_ch01_0748.summary: The section describes NuScale’s conformance with regulatory criteria regarding the inadvertent decrease in boron concentration in the reactor coolant system, specifically addressing fuel cladding integrity. It confirms that NuScale’s design meets the relevant requirements, noting that Critical Heat Flux (CHF) is the appropriate term for the phenomenon in their system, and that operator action is not required to mitigate such events.

edges.description: Section A provides a general overview of piping and instrumentation diagrams, while Section B specifies the subset of those diagrams that pertain specifically to electrical and instrumentation and control drawings, both referencing the same figures and tables for detailed design information.

Section A provides detailed engineering drawings and diagrams that visually clarify design information, which can be used as supporting evidence to demonstrate that the safety case and regulatory conformance claims in Section B are adequately addressed.

Section A provides detailed engineering drawings and diagrams that visually clarify design information, which can be used as supporting evidence to demonstrate conformance with the regulatory criteria and requirements referenced in Section B.

Section A provides detailed piping and instrumentation diagrams that visually clarify the design, which serve as supporting evidence for the regulatory conformance claims regarding steam system piping failures made in Section B.

Section A provides detailed engineering diagrams and tabular data that visually and technically clarify the design features referenced in Section B's general safety considerations for the CNV, thereby supporting the safety claims made in Section B.

Section A provides detailed electrical and I&C drawings and tables that visually clarify design information, which serve as supporting evidence for the regulatory conformance claims regarding radiation protection design features made in Section B.

Section A provides detailed electrical and I&C drawings and tables that visually clarify design information, which serve as supporting evidence for the regulatory conformance claims regarding radiation protection design features documented in Section B.

Section A provides detailed electrical and I&C drawings that visually clarify design information referenced in Section B, which documents conformance with regulatory criteria for steam system piping failures, thus supporting the safety case by supplying the engineering detail underlying regulatory compliance claims.

Section A provides detailed electrical and I&C drawings that visually clarify the design features supporting the safety considerations described in Section B, such as maintaining a vacuum in the CNV to minimize corrosion and facilitate leak detection.

Section A and Section B present identical tabular summaries of NuScale's conformance with specific radiation protection regulatory guides, directly referencing the same regulatory criteria and conformance statements, thus providing the same evidentiary basis for evaluating the adequacy of the safety case support by detailed engineering.

Section A lists the NuScale safety case's conformance and partial conformance with radiation protection regulatory guides, while Section B details the conformance of engineering analyses (e.g., steam system failures) with specific regulatory criteria, showing that the detailed engineering in Ch.05 provides the technical basis that supports the overall safety case claims in Ch.01.

Section A lists regulatory guidance and the degree of NuScale's conformance, while Section B describes specific design features (e.g., CNV vacuum) that serve as engineering implementations supporting the safety case referenced in Section A.

Section A lists the NuScale FSAR's conformance (full or partial) with radiation protection regulatory guides, while Section B details conformance with regulatory criteria for steam system piping failures, both referencing how the FSAR addresses specific DSRS and RG requirements, thus connecting through their documentation of regulatory compliance for different safety aspects.

Section A lists regulatory guidance and conformance statements regarding radiation protection design features, while Section B describes specific safety-related design features of the NuScale containment vessel (CNV) that contribute to overall plant safety, thus Section B provides detailed engineering evidence that supports the regulatory conformance claims made in Section A.

Section B describes the safety benefits of maintaining a vacuum in the CNV, which supports Section A's conformance statements regarding reactor coolant and main steam system pressure by providing engineering rationale for system reliability and leak detection.

Section A references the need for conformance with regulatory criteria for radiological consequence analyses, while Section B provides the detailed piping and instrumentation diagrams and tabular data that visually clarify and support the engineering basis for those analyses.

Section A references the regulatory conformance of radiological consequence analyses, including tabular data (e.g., Table 1), while Section B provides the detailed engineering drawings and tabular information (including Table 1) that visually clarify and support the design information referenced in Section A.

Section A (Ch.01 safety case conformance with radiological criteria) references and relies on the detailed radiation protection design features and regulatory conformance described in Section B (Ch.05), as evidenced by the cross-references to specific regulatory guides and FSAR sections.

Section A lists references to industry standards and guidelines that underpin the technical basis for reactor coolant boundary integrity, while Section B provides the detailed drawings and tabular data that visually and quantitatively represent the implementation of these standards in the NuScale design, thus Section B supports and operationalizes the requirements and guidance referenced in Section A.

Section A cites industry standards and guidelines that underpin the quality and reliability requirements for reactor coolant boundary integrity, while Section B provides the detailed electrical and instrumentation drawings that visually clarify how these requirements are implemented in the NuScale design.

Section A provides references to industry standards and guidelines that underpin the detailed engineering analyses in Chapter 5, while Section B documents how the overall safety case in Chapter 1 claims conformance or partial conformance with regulatory criteria, thus Section A supports the technical basis for the regulatory claims made in Section B.

Section A references the need for conformance with regulatory criteria for radiological analyses, while Section B provides the detailed piping and instrumentation diagrams that visually clarify and support the engineering basis underlying those analyses.

Section A references the need for conformance with regulatory criteria for radiological analyses, while Section B provides the detailed electrical and I&C drawings (including Table 1) that visually clarify and support the design information required to substantiate those analyses.

Section A (accident radiological consequence analyses and control room habitability) relies on the radiation protection design features detailed in Section B to demonstrate conformance with regulatory criteria for radiological safety, thus Section B provides supporting engineering evidence for the safety claims in Section A.

Section B provides detailed piping and instrumentation diagrams and tables that visually and technically clarify the design features referenced in Section A's discussion of communication systems and support centers, thereby supporting the adequacy of the safety case.

Section B provides detailed electrical and instrumentation control drawings that visually clarify and support the design provisions described in Section A regarding communication systems for emergency response centers, thereby offering engineering evidence for the safety case.

Both Section A and Section B document the NuScale FSAR's partial conformance with different DSRS criteria and associated regulatory guides, providing evidence of how specific safety-related systems (communication and radiation protection) align with regulatory requirements, which collectively support the overall safety case in Ch.01.

Section A (Ch.01 safety case conformance statements) references and relies on detailed engineering standards and supporting documents listed in Section B (Ch.05 references) to substantiate claims about reactor coolant boundary integrity and radiological habitability.

Section A references and summarizes the conformance of the overall safety case (including Technical Support Center radiological habitability) with regulatory criteria, while Section B provides the specific conformance details for control room radiological habitability, both connected through their reliance on DSRS 15.0.3 and cross-referencing of supporting FSAR sections.

Section A references the technical support center's radiological habitability as part of the overall safety case, while Section B specifies the communication system provisions and responsibilities for the technical support center, connecting them through the operational requirements for emergency response facilities.

Section A provides references to industry standards and technical guidelines that underpin the engineering details in Chapter 5, which are necessary to demonstrate conformance with the regulatory criteria and radiological consequence analyses described in Section B.

Section A lists references supporting the technical basis for reactor coolant boundary integrity, while Section B discusses conformance with regulatory criteria for communication systems and support centers, indicating that Section A provides supporting evidence for the safety case referenced in Section B.

Section A references Section B by citing Section 9.4 and 9.5 (which includes 9.5.2) as related to control room radiological habitability, indicating that communication systems (Section B) are part of the supporting systems considered in the overall safety case (Section A).

Section A establishes the regulatory criteria and conformance expectations for emergency facilities and equipment, while Section B provides the detailed engineering drawings and tabular data that visually clarify and specify how these criteria are implemented in the NuScale design.

Section A establishes regulatory criteria and conformance expectations for emergency facilities and equipment, while Section B provides the detailed electrical and I&C drawings that visually clarify and document how these criteria are implemented in the NuScale design.

Section A and Section B both document NuScale's conformance with regulatory criteria for different safety-related systems (fire protection and radiation protection), and both reference the use of tabular summaries (Table 1.9-2) to indicate the degree of conformance (e.g., partially conforms, not applicable) with specific regulatory guides.

Section A lists referenced topical and technical reports that provide foundational information for the safety case, while Section B describes the provision of detailed drawings and diagrams that visually clarify the engineering described in those reports, thus Section B supports and elaborates on the claims and references made in Section A.

Section A lists topical reports, such as those on quality assurance, that are referenced as part of the overall safety case, while Section B describes the provision of detailed electrical and I&C drawings that visually clarify the engineering aspects supporting the safety case, thus Section B provides detailed evidence that supports the claims and references in Section A.

Section A lists the topical and technical reports referenced in the NuScale FSAR, while Section B details the FSAR's conformance with specific regulatory criteria, indicating that the referenced materials in Section A provide supporting documentation for the regulatory compliance claims made in Section B.

Section B provides detailed engineering drawings and tabular data that visually and technically clarify the design information referenced in the overall safety case discussed in Section A, thereby supporting the adequacy of the safety case.

Section B provides detailed electrical and instrumentation/control drawings and tabular data that serve as engineering evidence to support the overall safety case claims made in Section A's summary and regulatory conformance statements.

Section A summarizes NuScale's conformance with operational and emergency planning regulatory criteria at a high level, while Section B provides detailed conformance information for radiation protection design features, both referencing the same Final Safety Analysis Report and its tables to demonstrate regulatory compliance.

Section A states that methods for measuring effective dose equivalent from external exposure are not applicable to NuScale and are the responsibility of the COL applicant or licensee, while Section B lists regulatory guidance for radiation protection design features and notes partial conformance or non-applicability, connecting both sections through their treatment of regulatory requirements for radiation protection and dose measurement responsibilities.

Section A discusses the applicability and partial conformance of regulatory criteria to the NuScale design certification, while Section B lists the specific topical and technical reports referenced in the safety analysis, connecting them by showing that the referenced materials in Section B are used to demonstrate or support the conformance claims made in Section A.

Section A discusses NuScale's partial conformance with fire protection and emergency communication system criteria as applicable to design certification, while Section B addresses the applicability and partial conformance of emergency planning and response facility requirements, connecting them through their shared focus on emergency preparedness and the extent to which NuScale's design supports regulatory safety case elements.

Section A discusses the applicability of regulatory guidance for emergency facilities and fire protection to the NuScale design certification, while Section B states that methods for measuring effective dose equivalent are not applicable to the design certification and are the responsibility of the COL applicant, indicating both sections clarify the scope of regulatory responsibilities between the design certification and COL applicant.

Section A lists topical and technical reports referenced in the NuScale FSAR, while Section B discusses the FSAR's conformance with specific regulatory criteria, indicating that the referenced materials in Section A provide supporting documentation for the regulatory claims made in Section B.
Section A's system evaluation relies on detailed diagrams and tabular data from Section B to clarify and substantiate the engineering basis for the safety case.
Section A references the detailed system drawings and figures described in Section B to support and clarify its evaluation of system performance and design parameters.

Section A provides detailed engineering evaluations and performance criteria for NuScale systems as described in Chapter 5, while Section B documents the conformance of these engineering features to regulatory radiation protection requirements referenced in Chapter 1, thus linking the detailed design to the overall safety case.

Section A outlines the regulatory criteria and conformance requirements for inspections, tests, and analyses, while Section B provides the detailed piping and instrumentation diagrams that serve as evidence to support and satisfy those regulatory requirements.

Section B provides detailed electrical and instrumentation drawings and tabular data that serve as engineering evidence supporting the overall safety case claims made in Section A, thereby linking the high-level safety assertions to concrete technical details.

Section A outlines the conformance of NuScale's structural and systems engineering inspections, tests, and acceptance criteria with regulatory criteria, while Section B details the conformance of radiation protection design features with regulatory guidance, both referencing the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report and its alignment with regulatory requirements.

Section A discusses the regulatory criteria and design features relevant to reactor coolant system safety, specifically noting the inapplicability of certain PWR requirements to NuScale due to its unique design, while Section B provides the detailed piping and instrumentation diagrams that visually clarify and support the design claims made in Section A.

Section B provides detailed electrical and instrumentation drawings that visually clarify and support the design information referenced in Section A's discussion of system conformance with regulatory criteria, thereby supporting the overall safety case analysis.

Section A and Section B both document NuScale's conformance with regulatory criteria by referencing specific SRP/DSRS sections and associated regulatory guides, but they address different technical areas (reactor coolant system vs. radiation protection), with their connection being that both contribute evidence toward the overall safety case evaluated in Ch.01.

Section A references the regulatory methodology and criteria (SRP 14.3.x) for inspections, tests, analyses, and acceptance criteria (ITAAC), which are supported by the detailed piping and instrumentation diagrams and drawings described in Section B as part of the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report.

Section A outlines the regulatory methodology and criteria (including ITAAC) that the NuScale safety case must conform to, while Section B provides the detailed electrical and I&C drawings and tabular data that serve as the engineering evidence supporting compliance with those criteria.

Section A outlines the general methodology and regulatory framework for inspections, tests, analyses, and acceptance criteria (ITAAC) that must be met, while Section B provides specific conformance details and partial compliance for radiation protection design features, thereby connecting the overall safety case requirements in Section A to the detailed engineering evidence in Section B.

Section A's system description of RCS functions and components is visually and functionally detailed in Section B's piping and instrumentation diagrams, which clarify and support the textual engineering claims.

Section A describes the functional roles and safety features of the RCS and its components, while Section B provides the supporting detailed drawings and tabular data that visually clarify and substantiate the engineering implementation of those features.

Section A provides detailed engineering descriptions of reactor coolant system features that are referenced in Section B as part of the overall safety case's conformance with radiation protection regulatory criteria.

Section A discusses the regulatory criteria and design features relevant to NuScale's reactor coolant system, specifically noting the absence of reactor coolant pumps, while Section B provides the detailed piping and instrumentation diagrams that visually clarify and support the design claims made in Section A.

Section A discusses the regulatory criteria and applicability of certain safety requirements to the NuScale design, while Section B provides the detailed electrical and I&C drawings that visually clarify and support the design information referenced in Section A, thus connecting regulatory conformance claims to specific engineering details.

Section A and Section B both reference NuScale's conformance with regulatory criteria in the Final Safety Analysis Report, with Section A focusing on reactor coolant system and fuel cladding integrity while Section B addresses radiation protection design features, thus both provide supporting evidence for the overall safety case in Ch.01 by detailing compliance with different regulatory requirements.

Section A provides detailed engineering evaluations and performance criteria for NuScale systems, which serve as supporting evidence for the regulatory conformance claims summarized in Section B regarding inspections, tests, and acceptance criteria.

Section A describes the system evaluation and operational criteria for the NuScale plant's steam generators and decay heat removal, while Section B discusses regulatory conformance and clarifies the inapplicability of certain SRP criteria due to NuScale's unique design (absence of reactor coolant pumps), thus Section A's engineering details provide the technical basis that supports Section B's regulatory justification.

Section A describes the system evaluation and performance criteria for the NuScale Power Plant's safety systems, while Section B lists regulatory criteria and methodologies (SRP/DSRS) that the safety analysis report must conform to, connecting them through the requirement that the engineering details in Section A must satisfy the regulatory acceptance criteria outlined in Section B.
Section A's system evaluation relies on the system description in Section B to establish the operational context and design features that are then assessed for performance and safety criteria.

Section A provides detailed engineering evaluations and performance criteria for NuScale systems, which serve as technical evidence supporting the overall safety case claims made in Section B regarding conformance with regulatory criteria.

Section A outlines the conformance of NuScale's safety analysis and test programs with regulatory criteria, while Section B discusses the applicability of specific accident scenarios and regulatory guidance to the NuScale design, both referencing how the NuScale safety case aligns with or diverges from standard regulatory expectations based on design features.

Section A outlines the conformance of NuScale's structural and systems engineering inspections, tests, analyses, and acceptance criteria (ITAAC) with regulatory criteria, while Section B addresses the conformance of human factors engineering and related ITAAC, both referencing the methodology provided in SRP 14.3 for developing ITAAC, thus connecting them through a shared regulatory framework for safety case substantiation.

Section A outlines regulatory conformance and testing requirements for structural and systems engineering, while Section B provides detailed engineering descriptions of the Reactor Coolant System (RCS), thereby allowing the detailed engineering in Section B to be evaluated against the regulatory criteria and acceptance criteria referenced in Section A.

Section A outlines the conformance of NuScale's safety analysis with regulatory criteria for inspections, tests, and acceptance criteria, while Section B addresses the applicability of specific accident scenarios (such as boron dilution events) to the NuScale design, clarifying that certain PWR-specific requirements do not apply due to design differences; thus, Section B provides context and justification that supports the adequacy of the safety case referenced in Section A by explaining why some regulatory requirements are not relevant.

Section A references regulatory criteria and exceptions based on the NuScale design's lack of reactor coolant pumps, while Section B provides detailed engineering descriptions of the Reactor Coolant System (RCS) that substantiate the design features referenced in Section A.

Section A addresses reactor coolant and main steam system pressures, while Section B addresses fuel cladding integrity, and both are connected by their evaluation of NuScale's conformance with regulatory criteria for inadvertent decrease in boron concentration, referencing the same SRP guidance and design features.

Section A outlines the regulatory criteria and methodologies (including ITAAC) that the NuScale safety case in Chapter 1 must conform to, while Section B provides specific engineering details from Chapter 5 (such as system descriptions and functions) that serve as evidence to demonstrate this conformance.

Section A outlines the general regulatory methodologies and acceptance criteria for inspections, tests, and analyses (ITAAC) that must be met, while Section B provides specific safety analysis criteria and exceptions for fuel cladding integrity in the context of the NuScale design, connecting them through the regulatory framework that ensures detailed engineering (Ch.05) aligns with the overall safety case (Ch.01).

Section A provides detailed engineering descriptions of the Reactor Coolant System (RCS) and its safety features, which serve as technical evidence supporting the overall safety case claims in Section B regarding conformance with regulatory criteria for fuel cladding integrity and system design.
